data.title: Boqii to list ordinary shares on NYSE American, replacing ADSs after reverse split
data.description:
• Boqii Holding (NYSE:BQ) will terminate its deposit agreement with The Bank of New York Mellon, ending its American Depositary Shares program.
  • In connection with the termination, Boqii will implement a 160-to-1 reverse stock split, consolidating its ordinary shares.
  • Boqii Holding Limited will list its Class A ordinary shares on NYSE American following the termination.
  • BQ shares down 4% post-market on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Raytheon awarded $279.2M U.S. Army contract
data.description:
• Raytheon (NYSE:RTX) has been awarded a $279.2M U.S. Army contract to provide functional management support for the land-based Phalanx Weapon System. 
  • Bids were solicited via the internet with one received. 
  • Work locations and funding will be determined with each order, with an estimated completion date of July 7, 2030. 
  • Army Contracting Command, Redstone Arsenal, Alabama, is the contracting activity.

data.title: Tesla Model Y completes first fully autonomous delivery
data.description:
• Tesla (NASDAQ:TSLA) completed its first fully autonomous vehicle delivery from factory to customer without the assistance of a safety driver or remote operator.
  • “The first fully autonomous delivery of a Tesla Model Y from factory to a customer home across town, including highways, was just completed a day ahead of schedule!!” CEO Elon Musk posted on X.
  • The car reached a maximum speed of 72 miles per hour, according to Tesla’s head of AI and autopilot, Ashok Elluswamy.
  • Musk had initially set his birthday, June 28, for the first autonomous delivery.

data.title: Applied Digital files to sell 14.06M shares of common stock for holders
data.description:

  • Filing

data.title: Titan Pharmaceuticals announces $600,000 private placement
data.description:
• Titan Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:TTNP) announced on Friday that it has completed a private placement of the company’s newly designated series C convertible preferred stock. 
  • Pursuant to the purchase agreement, Blue Harbour purchased 60,000 shares of preferred stock for an aggregate purchase price of $600,000. The shares have a conversion price of $3.40.
  • The certificate of designations authorizing the preferred stock contains a beneficial ownership conversion "blocker" that prevents Blue Harbour from acquiring the lower of either (i) the maximum percentage of common stock permissible under Nasdaq rules and regulations without first obtaining shareholder approval or (ii) 19.99% of the company's outstanding common stock.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: Xenon joins Russell 3000 and Russell 2000 indexes
data.description:
• Xenon Pharmaceuticals (NASDAQ:XENE) on Friday said the company has been added to the broad-market Russell 3000 and small-cap Russell 2000 Indexes as part of the annual reconstitution, effective at the close of U.S. equity markets on Friday, June 27, 2025.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: MercadoLibre snaps six days of gains as shares trade in red
data.description:
MercadoLibre (NASDAQ:MELI) shares snapped six straight days of gains as the stock closed 0.01% lower, at $2560.05 on Friday. 

The online marketplace company gained more than 6% in the last six trading days. The stock has gained more than 51% so far this year, compared to an over 4% gain in the broader benchmark index. 

MELI is down 1% over the past one month. The stock closed 0.78% higher, at $2560.20 on Thursday. 

Shares of MercadoLibre have been under pressure since early this month after the e-commerce giant said it lowered the threshold for free shipping in Brazil to compete with rival Amazon in the Latin American market.

The company will now offer free shipping on orders of $3.40 or more from the prior minimum of $14.15.

According to Bank of America, MercadoLibre's actions to reduce seller shipping fees and offer wider free-shipping should attract more buyers and sellers to the platform, as well as increase the overall level of inventory.

Analyst Robert Aguilar said an increase in buying frequency and more trials of new categories could MercadoLibre's gross merchandise volume and increase route density, which would also push margins higher.

Seeking Alpha’s Quant rating system has rated the stock HOLD, while SA authors and sell-side analysts have rated the stock BUY.

data.title: Micron 10-Q reveals one customer accounted for 16% of revenue: Wells Fargo
data.description:
Micron Technology (NASDAQ:MU) recently filed its 10-Q for its fiscal third-quarter, and Wells Fargo pointed out that one customer accounted for a significant portion of its revenue. 

“MU's 10-Q discloses that it had one 10%+ customer during the 9-mos ending F3Q25—accounting for 16% of cumulative [9-month ended May revenue],” analyst Aaron Rakers wrote in a note to clients. “This compares to a disclosed ~15% customer for 1HFY25. Assuming this is the same customer, this would imply ~$1.7 billion in F3Q25 revenue, vs. our estimate of ~$1.1B and ~ $1.4B in F1Q25 and F2Q25.”

The filing adds that the unnamed customer is primarily included in Micron's Compute and Networking Business Unit. This implies that the customer accounts for roughly 25% to 30% of Micron's Compute and Networking Business Unit revenue, Rakers added.

Other notable takeaways from the 10-Q include a decline in customer prepays, as contract liabilities from customer prepayments fell to around $146M at the end of the quarter, compared to $614M and $555M in the first and second quarters; and the receipt of $266M in government incentives to offset capex, compared to $963M in the first quarter of fiscal 2025 and $33M in the year-ago quarter.

“The company's liabilities include a $603M balance in non-current unearned [government] incentives vs. $836M in the prior [quarter],” Rakers added.

data.title: Cineverse outlines multi-film release slate and targets AI-driven growth following 58% revenue increase
data.description:
Earnings Call Insights: Cineverse Corp. (CNVS) Q4 2025

Management View

  • CEO Christopher J. McGurk highlighted that the fourth quarter continued "very strong financial and business momentum," with total revenue of $15.6 million and net income of $858,000, both representing considerable increases over the prior year. He stressed, "we've continued that very strong financial and business momentum in our fourth fiscal quarter, generating impressive growth in all our financial performance measures and beating consensus analyst guidance on all key metrics."
  • McGurk emphasized the strategic reorganization, stating the company has "set up our technology business as a separate business group and put Tony Huidor in charge as President and Chief Product Officer" to capitalize on proprietary streaming content management and AI technology. He also introduced the creation of a dedicated Theatrical Motion Pictures division, led by Yolanda Macias as Chief Motion Pictures Officer, focusing on building a wide release slate modeled after the successful Terrifier franchise.
  • The next wide release, The Toxic Avenger, is set for August 29. McGurk explained the company’s investment in each upcoming film will be "less than $5 million," and "the film will generate approximately the same level of financial return to Cineverse as Terrifier 3 if it performs at only half of Terrifier's box office level."
  • CFO Mark Wayne Lindsey stated, "We are able to beat analyst consensus estimates for revenue, net income, diluted EPS and adjusted EBITDA for both the fourth quarter and the full year." He reported revenues of $15.6 million for the quarter, net income of $0.9 million, and adjusted EBITDA of $4 million, calling attention to a direct operating margin of 55%. SG&A expenses fell to $5.4 million, or 35% of revenues. "We had $13.9 million in cash and cash equivalents on our balance sheet as of March 31, 2025 with $0 outstanding on our $12.5 million working capital facility."
  • President and Chief Strategy Officer Erick Opeka noted that Screambox subscribers grew 31% since the Home Premier of Terrifier 3, and the streaming business saw "over 3.2 billion minutes streamed across our owned and operated services, up 45% over the prior year."

Outlook

  • Management plans at least three wide release films in the current fiscal year, each with investments below $5 million and with a focus on franchise IP properties. Upcoming releases include The Toxic Avenger, Silent Night, Deadly Night, and Return to Silent Hill.
  • McGurk stated, "Expect more wide release film announcements in the next few months as we build our slate for fiscal year 2026."
  • CFO Lindsey expects direct operating margin "to remain in the 45% to 50% range" for future quarters and anticipates SG&A expenses will continue to decline as a percentage of revenues.
  • Opeka said the company plans to expand the new Cineverse streaming channel beyond Amazon and will "focus our investments in growth on these 4 properties in the short to midterm"—Screambox, Dove, Fandor, and Midnight Pulp.

Financial Results

  • The fourth quarter reported revenue was $15.6 million, with net income of $0.9 million and adjusted EBITDA of $4 million. The direct operating margin was 55%, above the company's target range.
  • SG&A expenses decreased to $5.4 million, or 35% of revenues. Cash and cash equivalents stood at $13.9 million as of March 31, 2025, with $0 outstanding on the $12.5 million working capital facility and a working capital surplus of $3.6 million.
  • For the year, total revenues grew to $78.2 million, net income reached $3.8 million, and adjusted EBITDA was $13.9 million. Net cash provided by operations was $18.5 million for the year.

Q&A

  • Daniel Louis Kurnos, The Benchmark Company: "Chris, high level, just if you guys are successful or see early signs of success, how much more are you willing to lean in?" CEO McGurk responded that more films following the Terrifier blueprint would be announced, with expansions into family, fantasy, Black Cinema, and comedy. "Once we put those pieces into our release slate, I think that's when we'll get really serious about negotiating a pay deal."
  • Kurnos also asked about operating margin potential. CFO Lindsey replied, "margin last quarter was 49%." McGurk added, "we feel good about the 55% number that we put up, and we feel really good that we'll meet or exceed our operating margin target of 45% to 50% going forward."
  • Brian David Kinstlinger, Alliance Global Partners, inquired about the pipeline and deal sizes for Matchpoint and cineSearch. Mark Antonio Huidor indicated that "each of those studios is probably $5 million and up...So that $5 million can easily scale much higher." Opeka added, "the reception has been very, very robust."
  • Kinstlinger asked about podcast monetization. Opeka stated, "podcast CPMs, higher than CTV CPMs, sometimes by even $10 or more higher on a direct basis" and that average deal sizes are "low 6 figures per deal."

Sentiment Analysis

  • Analysts were broadly positive, focusing on the company’s slate expansion, technology pipeline, and monetization strategies. Their questions highlighted interest in scalability, profitability, and new business lines.
  • Management maintained a confident tone throughout, repeatedly referencing strong performance and future opportunities. McGurk emphasized, "we feel really good that we'll meet or exceed our operating margin target."
  • Compared to the previous quarter, both management and analysts exhibited similarly optimistic sentiment, though this quarter saw increased emphasis on AI and technology-driven growth opportunities.

Quarter-over-Quarter Comparison

  • Guidance language remained consistent, with management reiterating operating margin targets and the focus on cost control. The strategic focus shifted toward the creation of dedicated entertainment and technology divisions, with a new emphasis on AI and the Matchpoint platform.
  • The prior quarter emphasized rapid growth from the Terrifier 3 release, while the current quarter broadened the focus to a diversified slate and technology commercialization.
  • Key metrics such as revenue, net income, and margin continued to show improvement, though at lower levels due to seasonality as outlined by management.
  • Analysts’ focus shifted from individual film economics to scalability of new technology products and recurring revenue streams.
  • Management’s confidence in the sustainability of margins and future revenue growth was sustained and reinforced.

Risks and Concerns

  • CFO Lindsey noted, "the depressed direct and programmatic advertising environment in the first quarter, which is a direct result of companies pulling back on their discretionary advertising spend due to the ever-changing tariff environment."
  • Opeka described a "glut of supply with huge growth in available competitive channels from studios outstripping the audience growth in the market," which has put pressure on CPMs and fill rates for programmatic advertising.
  • Huidor noted that major studios "still rely on manual workflows, fragmented system, a patchwork of external vendors and massive internal teams," which presents both a challenge and opportunity for Matchpoint.

Final Takeaway

Cineverse Corp. delivered another quarter of strong revenue and margin growth, supported by a diversified film slate, record streaming engagement, and a newly structured focus on technology and AI. The company’s outlook is anchored by upcoming wide releases, robust technology commercialization opportunities with Matchpoint and cineSearch, and continued cost discipline. Management is confident in its ability to sustain high margins, expand its addressable market, and drive long-term value through strategic investments in content and technology.
